name: Custom 1 — Harbor Assault
_notes: EXAMPLE custom level. It reuses stage1's harbor geometry + camera path via "baseStage": "stage1" and supplies its own, tougher encounters — same place, your fight. TO MAKE YOUR OWN: copy this file to levels/<yourid>.json, give it a unique "id" (becomes the menu button + start card), set "baseStage" to stage1 | stage2 | stage3, then edit "waves". It appears in the menu automatically. Wave/enemy semantics: each enemy "position" is a CAMERA-RELATIVE offset resolved at spawn time — x = right, -z = ahead, y = 0 (ground raycast). Keep z around -12..-21 for face-to-face engagement. "time" is seconds along the camera path; put a matching entry in "clearPoints" so the camera HALTS there until the wave is cleared (an arena). The base stage's camera DWELL points make the cleanest, unoccluded arenas — for stage1 the verified-clean ones are 4 / 56 / 74 / 112 / 160 / 173s (boss ~178, path loops ~196). For other stages find dwell points with tools/extract-stage-assets/analyze-path.mjs and EYEBALL them in preview (some dwell points face a wall — pick open ones), then check spawns land in view with verify-waves.mjs. enemy "type": grunt (hp1), gunman (hp2, shoots harder), heavy (hp4), innocent (civilian — do NOT shoot, costs a life).
